title: Cyperus esphacelatus
taxonomy: Cyperus esphacelatus is classified under the kingdom Plantae, the family Cyperaceae, and the genus Cyperus. It is a type of sedge that belongs to the monocot group of flowering plants. Cyperus esphacelatus is further categorized under the order Poales and the class Liliopsida. The species Cyperus esphacelatus is also known as the umbrella sedge, named for its unique umbrella-like seed heads. The classification of Cyperus esphacelatus is crucial for its identification and understanding of its ecological role.

intro: Cyperus esphacelatus, also known as Chinese flat sedge, is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which help to treat conditions such as fever, sore throat, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Cyperus esphacelatus include saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in the form of powder or capsules to treat various health conditions.
